title: pilosella glaucescens subsp. megalocymophora
side_effects: Pilosella glaucescens subsp. megalocymophora can have side effects if used improperly, such as gastrointestinal irritation, nausea, and diarrhea due to its high content of sesquiterpene lactones. In some cases, prolonged exposure to these compounds can also cause allergic reactions, including skin rashes and itching. Additionally, consuming large quantities of the plant may lead to liver and kidney damage, as its toxic compounds can accumulate in the body and cause oxidative stress. It is essential to exercise caution and consult with a medical professional before using Pilosella glaucescens subsp. megalocymophora for medicinal purposes.
